title: aquilegia coerulea var. coerulea
intro: Aquilegia coerulea var. coerulea, also known as the Colorado blue columbine, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is prized for its delicate, sky-blue flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Aquilegia coerulea var. coerulea can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are said to offer additional health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues.
